docs.title: Home
docs.text:
ATOMS + QUARKS

QUARKS Survey

A high-resolution ALMA Band 6 view of massive protoclusters, building on the ATOMS Band 3 legacy.

QUARKS follows dense protoclusters from the ATOMS sample to smaller physical scales, combining target catalogs, continuum products, and multi-scale image atlases to study fragmentation, core formation, gas kinematics, accretion flows, feedback, and the early evolution of massive star-forming regions.

Browse Targets Open Atlas 139 QUARKS targets 156 Band 6 pointings ATOMS Band 3 survey legacy ~1000 au resolved physical scales

Survey Scope

Connecting clump-scale environments to protocluster-scale structure

ATOMS established a uniform ALMA Band 3 view of active massive star-forming regions. QUARKS extends this foundation with higher-resolution Band 6 observations, targeting the internal structure and kinematics of dense protoclusters.

This website is designed as the public entry point for QUARKS products: target information, continuum images and FITS files, source-by-source multi-scale atlases, publications, team information, and contact points for collaboration.

Legacy How the ATOMS Band 3 survey motivates and supports the QUARKS Band 6 program. Targets Basic source properties for the 139-object QUARKS target sample. Continuum Browse continuum images and download corresponding continuum FITS products. Atlas Multi-scale, multi-wavelength image atlas for individual sources. Publications Papers and science results based on ATOMS and QUARKS observations. Contact Find contact information for data questions and collaboration requests.

docs.title: Atlas
docs.text:
The Atlas section will host a multi-scale and multi-wavelength image atlas for individual QUARKS sources.

Each target will eventually have a corresponding visual product that combines the broader ATOMS context with the higher-resolution QUARKS view and other wavelength information when available. The current atlas draft is organized as one source per page in QUARKS_ATLAS_new.pdf; the website version will be converted into a browsable source-by-source atlas.

Planned atlas entries:

  • source name and target identifier
  • multi-scale image panel
  • links to continuum products when available
  • links to related publications or notes

This page will be expanded after the final atlas file organization is fixed.

docs.title: Contact us
docs.text: If you have any questions, please feel free to contact astrojiao@gmail.com and liutie@shao.ac.cn

docs.title: Datapolicy
docs.text: Please add the following sentences in Acknowledgments: This paper uses the following ALMA data: ADS/JAO. ALMA#2021.1.00095.S. ALMA is a partnership of ESO (representing its member states), NSF (USA) and NINS (Japan), together with NRC (Canada), MOST and ASIAA (Taiwan, China), and KASI (Republic of Korea), in cooperation with the Republic of Chile. The Joint ALMA Observatory is operated by ESO, AUI/NRAO, and NAOJ. The MeerKAT telescope is operated by the South African Radio Astronomy Observatory, which is a facility of the National Research Foundation, an agency of the Department of Science and Innovation.

docs.title: ATOMS as the foundation for QUARKS
docs.text:
ATOMS, the ALMA Three-millimeter Observations of Massive Star-forming regions survey, provides a Band 3 view of active massive star-forming regions. It established the large-scale context for dense gas structures, molecular line emission, feedback signatures, and the environments in which massive protoclusters form.

QUARKS builds on this legacy by following the target sample to higher angular resolution with ALMA Band 6 observations. Together, ATOMS and QUARKS connect clump-scale gas structures to protocluster-scale fragmentation, dense core formation, accretion flows, outflows, chemistry, and feedback.

docs.title: Why combine ATOMS and QUARKS?
docs.text:
• ATOMS provides the broader Band 3 view of massive star-forming environments.
  • QUARKS resolves denser, smaller-scale Band 6 structures within the same evolutionary context.
  • The combined data set enables source-by-source multi-scale comparisons across continuum and line products.
  • The atlas products on this website are intended to make those comparisons easier to inspect and cite.

More detailed references and links will be collected in the Publications section.

docs.title: Publications
docs.text:
Literature

Publications QUARKS Series

Core papers presenting the QUARKS survey and first science results.

  1. QUARKS Paper I Introduces the QUARKS survey design, target sample, observing strategy, and the first high-resolution ALMA Band 6 view of dense protoclusters selected from the ATOMS legacy.
  2. QUARKS Paper II Presents early QUARKS science enabled by the Band 6 data products, focusing on the internal structure and physical conditions of massive protocluster targets.
  3. QUARKS Paper III Extends the QUARKS survey series with new analysis of dense structures and source evolution across the high-resolution target sample.
QUARKS-related Papers

Papers using QUARKS products or closely connected follow-up analyses.

  1. Evolution of starless cores in massive clumps seen by the ALMA ASHES and QUARKS surveys 2026 Jun · Yang, Dongting; Liu, Hong-li; Qin, Sheng-li et al. Compares ASHES and QUARKS samples to trace how starless cores evolve in massive clump environments.
  2. Tracking Protostellar Variability in Massive Protoclusters with ALMA. I. Insights from QUARKS and MaMMOtH 2026 May · Yang, Yuhan; Liu, Tie; Liu, Sheng-Yuan et al. Uses ALMA data to investigate variability signatures in massive protocluster environments.
  3. ALMA-QUARKS view of W49N: Multipolar episodic outflow associated with the most energetic Galactic water maser 2026 Apr · Jiao, Yunfan; Liu, Tie; Jiao, Wenyu et al. Resolves complex episodic outflow activity in W49N and links it to powerful water maser emission.
  4. The ALMA-QUARKS Survey: Evidence of an Explosive Molecular Outflow in IRAS 15520-5234 2026 Apr · Hoque, Ariful; Baug, Tapas; Guzman, Estrella et al. Reports an explosive molecular outflow candidate revealed by QUARKS observations.
  5. The ALMA-QUARKS survey: Investigating Thermal Feedback of Massive Protostars in Hot Molecular Cores 2026 Mar · Meng, Dezhao; Liu, Tie; Esimbek, Jarken et al. Examines how massive protostars heat and reshape their surrounding hot molecular cores.
  6. ATOMS-QUARKS Survey: Inflow and Infall in Massive Protocluster G318.049+00.086 2026 Mar · Gupta, Shivani; Baug, Tapas; Soam, Archana et al. Combines ATOMS and QUARKS views to identify inflow and infall signatures linked to competitive accretion.
  7. The ALMA-QUARKS Survey: Hot Molecular Cores Are a Long-standing Phenomenon in the Evolution of Massive Protostars Meng, Dezhao; Liu, Tie; Esimbek, Jarken et al. Studies hot-core occurrence across massive protostellar evolution using QUARKS diagnostics.
  8. ALMA-QUARKS: Few-thousand-year "Hatching out of the Egg" 2026 Feb · Zhang, Siju; Garay, Guido; Xu, Fengwei et al. Interprets a hypercompact H II region breaking out from its parental hot core on a short timescale.
  9. The ALMA-QUARKS Survey: Evidence of a Candidate High-mass Prestellar Core Aside a Bright-rimmed Cloud IRAS 18290-0924 2025 Dec · Yang, Dongting; Liu, Hong-Li; Qin, Sheng-li et al. Identifies and analyzes a candidate high-mass prestellar core in a feedback-affected environment.
  10. The ALMA-ATOMS-QUARKS Survey: Resolving a Chemically Rich Massive Protostellar Outflow 2025 Dec · Zou, Jiahang; Liu, Tie; Xu, Fengwei et al. Links outflow structure and chemistry using combined ATOMS and QUARKS observations.
  11. The ALMA-QUARKS survey: Extensive detection of acetamide in multiple high-mass star-forming regions 2025 Oct · Duan, Chunguo; Xu, Xuefang; Gou, Qian et al. Reports widespread acetamide detections and probes complex chemistry in high-mass star-forming regions.
  12. The ALMA-QUARKS Survey: Fibers' Role in Star Formation Unveiled in an Intermediate-mass Protocluster Region of the Vela D Cloud Yang, Dongting; Liu, Hong-Li; Liu, Tie et al. Explores how fiber-like gas structures participate in star formation within Vela D.
  13. The ALMA-QUARKS Survey: Detection of Two Extremely Dense Substructures in a Massive Prestellar Core QUARKS-related dense-core study Highlights compact, very dense substructures that may represent early stages of massive core evolution.
Legacy: ATOMS Series

The ATOMS survey provides the Band 3 legacy foundation for QUARKS. The full ATOMS literature is collected through ADS.

Open ATOMS literature in ADS

docs.title: Team
docs.text:
Collaboration

QUARKS Team

The QUARKS collaboration brings together researchers working on massive star formation, ALMA observations, dense gas kinematics, fragmentation, chemistry, feedback, and multi-scale data products.

Consortium Members

Names are sorted alphabetically by family name.
